home.benefit.remote.title.message: Pick up the current work without guesswork

home.benefit.remote.body.message: Open the work already running on your Mac from the list, so you can recover context quickly without hunting for the right place to start.

home.benefit.setup.title.message: Connect with a QR code on the same LAN

home.benefit.setup.body.message: Open the Mac app, show the QR code, and scan it from iPhone. Adding your Mac takes just a few steps.

home.benefit.context.title.message: Keep everything organized by work item

home.benefit.context.body.message: Conversations, branches, skills, and commands stay grouped around each work item, so it is easier to keep track of which environment you are touching.

home.step.bridge.body.message: Prepare the QR code and the work list in the Mac app.

home.step.pair.body.message: Import it with a QR code or setup code, then wait for the connection check and work list to load.

home.step.resume.title.message: Open the work and continue from there

home.step.resume.body.message: Open the work, follow the conversation, add a quick follow-up from the input box, or start a new conversation when you need one.

home.scenario.commute.title.message: You want to check progress from the couch

home.scenario.commute.body.message: Leave the longer task running on the Mac and open the conversation on iPhone to check progress first.

home.scenario.feature.sofa.alt.message: Illustration of using CodexPocket from a smartphone while sitting on a couch

home.scenario.nightcare.title.message: You want to send one quick follow-up after putting the kids to bed

home.scenario.nightcare.body.message: Send the quick follow-up first after the kids are asleep, and leave heavier work and log checking for the Mac.

home.scenario.feature.nightcare.alt.message: Illustration of using CodexPocket from a smartphone in bed after the kids are asleep

home.scenario.breaktime.title.message: You want to send one quick follow-up during a break

home.scenario.breaktime.body.message: Even between videos or games, you can send the follow-up first from your iPhone and leave execution and log management to the Mac workflow.

home.scenario.feature.breaktime.alt.message: Illustration of using CodexPocket from a smartphone while relaxing

home.guide.iphone.body.message: The best starting point if you want to learn how to add a Mac, browse work, read conversations, and send follow-ups from the input box.

home.guide.mac.body.message: The best starting point if you want to understand the Mac app, connection layer, QR setup, and logs first.

home.guide.shared.body.message: A dedicated section for topics that cross the boundary between iPhone and Mac.

home.guide.reference.body.message: The place to look for cross-cutting information such as release policy and multilingual support.

home.meta.title.message: Resume Codex on your Mac from your iPhone

home.meta.description.message: CodexPocket is an iPhone app that lets you resume Codex running on your Mac without going back to your desk.

home.hero.title.message: Resume the Codex running on your Mac from your iPhone.

home.hero.lead.message: Do not break the flow just because you stepped away from your desk. CodexPocket keeps the Codex environment on your Mac and lets you open work, check status, and send a quick follow-up from your iPhone, so you can clear short checks before you get back to the Mac.

home.hero.meta.public.message: Mac version {stableVersion} is available now. Once you install the iPhone app, your first connection is just a QR scan away on the same LAN.

home.hero.meta.unavailable.message: Mac version {stableVersion} is available now. There is no public App Store or TestFlight link for the iPhone app yet, so check the current availability first.

home.hero.note.newThread.message: Execution and logs stay on the Mac

home.hero.note.sync.message: Use iPhone for checks and light follow-ups

home.visual.title.message: Control from iPhone, execution on Mac. That makes it easy to stay in the flow when you step away.

home.visual.body.message: The Mac app and connection layer keep the work anchored on the Mac, while the iPhone gives you a quick way back in whenever you need it.

home.visual.action.value.message: Start a new conversation from the input box

home.visual.panelTitle.message: What you can do from iPhone

home.visual.panel.project.body.message: Jump into the right conversation or branch

home.visual.panel.composer.body.message: Send follow-ups, use skills, and trigger one-off runs

home.limitations.lan.body.message: The main goal is a quick resume when the Mac and iPhone are on the same LAN.

home.limitations.realtime.title.message: Execution and source of truth stay on the Mac

home.limitations.realtime.body.message: Connection handling, logs, and work management stay on the Mac, while the iPhone focuses on checking and input.

home.limitations.future.title.message: Not built for always-on from-anywhere access

home.limitations.future.body.message: It fits best when you want to make short interactions easier after stepping away, not when you want a permanent remote setup from anywhere.

home.section.benefits.title.message: Lower the cost of interruption when you step away from your desk

home.section.limits.title.message: It lands best when these assumptions match
